comment: Using node and property name mapping: technique and id, name, description

text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Explain how campaign {c:campaign.name} ends up using technique {t.name}.

Answer: 
Campaign {c.name} is attributed to intrusion set {is:c.attributed-to.name}, which uses malware {m:is.uses>malware.name}, which uses attack-pattern {t:m.uses.name}.

Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Explain how technique {t:technique.name} ({t.id}) is used both by tool {tl.name} and campaign {c.name} ({c.id}).
Answer: Technique {t} ({t.id}) is used by tool {tl:t.used-by>tool.name} and it is also used by malware {m:t.uses<malware.name} (ID {m.id}). Malware {m.id} is used directly by campaign {c:m.uses<campaign} (id {c.id}).
text-quick: technique {t.name}  {t.id} tool {tl.name} campaign {c.name} ({c.id}) technique {t} ({t.id}) tool {tl:t.used-by>tool.name} malware {m:t.uses<malware.name} (ID {m.id}) Malware {m.id} campaign {c:m.uses<campaign} (id {c.id}).

text-long:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: What are the name and ID of a mitigation that mitigates technique {t:technique.name} (id {t.id}) on platforms {t.x_mitre_platforms} that is also used by malware {m:t.uses<malware.name}? 
Answer: Mitigation {coa:t.mitigates<.name} mitigates technique {t.name}.
text:
technique {t:technique.name} (id {t.id}) platforms {t.x_mitre_platforms} malware {m:t.uses<malware.name} 
Answer: Mitigation {coa:t.mitigates<.name} technique {t.name}.

Question:  
Answer: Mitigation {coa:t.mitigates<.name} mitigates technique {t.name}.
text: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} (on Windows: platforms={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']}) and technique {t2:tl.uses.name} (on Linux: platforms={t2.x_mitre_platforms[?='Linux']}), both mitigated by mitigations {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}.

comment: Invisible Section: Filter for non-list property, #2
text-long:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question:  
Answer: Mitigation {coa:t.mitigates<.name} mitigates technique {t.name}.
text: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} <*(on Windows: platforms={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']})*> and technique {t2:tl.uses.name} <*(on Linux: platforms={t2.x_mitre_platforms[?='Linux']})*>, both mitigated by mitigations {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}.

Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: Explain how tool {tl:tool.name} uses a technique that works on Windows and another that works only on Linux, both techniques having the same course of action.
Answer: Tool {tl.name} uses two different techniques, {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}. Both techniques are mitigated by mitigation {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name}<*INVISIBLE: {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name} constraint {force coa1.id=coa2.id}*>. Technique {t1.name} works on platforms (inclding Windows): {t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']} and technique t2 works only on platform <* ONLY LINUX*>{t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}).
text-short: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}, both mitigated by mitigations (same!) {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}. Constraint {force coa1.id=coa2.id}. t1 platforms(?Win)={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']}) and technique t2 platform(*Linux) {t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}).

text:
Instruction: You are a CTI expert that gives precise and concise answers.

Question: How could course of action {coa1:course-of-action.name} (id {coa1.id}) prevent achieving tactic {ta1.name} (id {ta1.id})?
Answer: mitigation or tactic {coa1.name} mitigates attack pattern {ap1:coa1.mitigates.name}, which achieves tactic {ta1:ap1.achieves.name} (id {ta1.id}).
Modification times: 
coa1: {coa1.modified}
ap1: {ap1.modified}
ta1: {ta1.modified}. <*{coa1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]}  {ap1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]} {ta1.modified[_>=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z]} *>
text-short: Tool {tl:tool.name} uses technique {t1:tl.uses.name} and technique {t2:tl.uses.name}, both mitigated by mitigations (same!) {coa1:t1.mitigates<.name} / {coa2:t2.mitigates<.name}. Constraint {force coa1.id=coa2.id}. t1 platforms(?Win)={t1.x_mitre_platforms[?='Windows']}) and technique t2 platform(*Linux) {t2.x_mitre_platforms[*='Linux']}).

comment: VERSIONING: used global constraint on modified datetime using generation config field. See below.
comment 2: 'apply_to': 'all' means that the given date constraint applies to ALL nodes involved, incuding intermediary
comment 3: 'apply_to': 'any' means that the given date constraint applies to AT LEAST one node involved in the query path solution, incuding intermediary nodoes

modified_constraints.comment: after, before, or none bay be omitted

order_by_ALT: other options: var.prop, var.prop, ..., random, none
